name: Strings
javadoc:
/**
 * Static utility methods pertaining to {@code String} or {@code CharSequence}
 * instances.
 *
 * @author Kevin Bourrillion
 * @since 3.0
 */

members.name: nullToEmpty
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the given string if it is non-null; the empty string otherwise.
 *
 * @param string the string to test and possibly return
 * @return {@code string} itself if it is non-null; {@code ""} if it is null
 */

tags.name: string
tags.content: the string to test and possibly return
tags.description: the string to test and possibly return

tags.content: string} itself if it is non-null; {@code ""} if it is null
tags.description: Returns the given string if it is non-null; the empty string otherwise.

members.name: emptyToNull
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the given string if it is nonempty; {@code null} otherwise.
 *
 * @param string the string to test and possibly return
 * @return {@code string} itself if it is nonempty; {@code null} if it is
 *     empty or null
 */

tags.name: string
tags.content: the string to test and possibly return
tags.description: the string to test and possibly return

tags.content: string} itself if it is nonempty; {@code null} if it is empty or null
tags.description: Returns the given string if it is nonempty; null otherwise.

members.name: isNullOrEmpty
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns {@code true} if the given string is null or is the empty string. * *

Consider normalizing your string references with {@link #nullToEmpty}. * If you do, you can use {@link String#isEmpty()} instead of this * method, and you won't need special null-safe forms of methods like {@link * String#toUpperCase} either. Or, if you'd like to normalize "in the other * direction," converting empty strings to {@code null}, you can use {@link * #emptyToNull}. * * @param string a string reference to check * @return {@code true} if the string is null or is the empty string */

tags.content: true} if the string is null or is the empty string
tags.description: true} if the string is null or is the empty string

members.code:
/** * Returns {@code true} if the given string is null or is the empty string. * *

Consider normalizing your string references with {@link #nullToEmpty}. * If you do, you can use {@link String#isEmpty()} instead of this * method, and you won't need special null-safe forms of methods like {@link * String#toUpperCase} either. Or, if you'd like to normalize "in the other * direction," converting empty strings to {@code null}, you can use {@link * #emptyToNull}. * * @param string a string reference to check * @return {@code true} if the string is null or is the empty string */ public static boolean isNullOrEmpty(@Nullable String string) { // string.isEmpty() in Java 6 return string == null || string.length() == 0; }

members.name: padStart
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a string, of length at least {@code minLength}, consisting of * {@code string} prepended with as many copies of {@code padChar} as are * necessary to reach that length. For example, * *
    *
  • {@code padStart("7", 3, '0')} returns {@code "007"} *
  • {@code padStart("2010", 3, '0')} returns {@code "2010"} *
* *

See {@link Formatter} for a richer set of formatting capabilities. * * @param string the string which should appear at the end of the result * @param minLength the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be * zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned. * @param padChar the character to insert at the beginning of the result until * the minimum length is reached * @return the padded string */

tags.name: string
tags.content: the string which should appear at the end of the result
tags.description: the string which should appear at the end of the result

tags.name: minLength
tags.content: the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned.
tags.description: the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned.

tags.name: padChar
tags.content: the character to insert at the beginning of the result until the minimum length is reached
tags.description: the character to insert at the beginning of the result until the minimum length is reached

tags.assertion: assert methodResultID.length() >= args[1] && methodResultID.startsWith(String.valueOf(args[2]).repeat(Math.max(0, args[1] - String.valueOf(args[2]).length())) + args[0]);
tags.description: Returns a string, of length at least minLength, consisting of string prepended with as many copies of padChar as are necessary to reach that length.

members.name: padEnd
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a string, of length at least {@code minLength}, consisting of * {@code string} appended with as many copies of {@code padChar} as are * necessary to reach that length. For example, * *
    *
  • {@code padEnd("4.", 5, '0')} returns {@code "4.000"} *
  • {@code padEnd("2010", 3, '!')} returns {@code "2010"} *
* *

See {@link Formatter} for a richer set of formatting capabilities. * * @param string the string which should appear at the beginning of the result * @param minLength the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be * zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned. * @param padChar the character to append to the end of the result until the * minimum length is reached * @return the padded string */

tags.name: string
tags.content: the string which should appear at the beginning of the result
tags.description: the string which should appear at the beginning of the result

tags.name: minLength
tags.content: the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned.
tags.description: the minimum length the resulting string must have. Can be zero or negative, in which case the input string is always returned.

tags.name: padChar
tags.content: the character to append to the end of the result until the minimum length is reached
tags.description: the character to append to the end of the result until the minimum length is reached

members.name: repeat
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns a string consisting of a specific number of concatenated copies of
 * an input string. For example, {@code repeat("hey", 3)} returns the string
 * {@code "heyheyhey"}.
 *
 * @param string any non-null string
 * @param count the number of times to repeat it; a nonnegative integer
 * @return a string containing {@code string} repeated {@code count} times
 *     (the empty string if {@code count} is zero)
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code count} is negative
 */

tags.name: count
tags.content: the number of times to repeat it; a nonnegative integer
tags.description: the number of times to repeat it; a nonnegative integer

tags.content: string containing {@code string} repeated {@code count} times (the empty string if {@code count} is zero)
tags.description: a string containing the input string repeated the specified number of times

members.name: commonPrefix
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the longest string {@code prefix} such that
 * {@code a.toString().startsWith(prefix) && b.toString().startsWith(prefix)},
 * taking care not to split surrogate pairs. If {@code a} and {@code b} have
 * no common prefix, returns the empty string.
 *
 * @since 11.0
 */

members.name: commonSuffix
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the longest string {@code suffix} such that
 * {@code a.toString().endsWith(suffix) && b.toString().endsWith(suffix)},
 * taking care not to split surrogate pairs. If {@code a} and {@code b} have
 * no common suffix, returns the empty string.
 *
 * @since 11.0
 */

members.name: validSurrogatePairAt
members.javadoc:
/**
 * True when a valid surrogate pair starts at the given {@code index} in the
 * given {@code string}. Out-of-range indexes return false.
 */
